title: Ericsson reports 10% sales drop in Q1, backs off North America
summary: The outlook got a little darker for Ericsson, which today reported a 10% year-over-year decrease in reported net sales in Q1, to about $5.4 billion.

title: Ericsson employees in Sweden, US and Japan trial 5G laptops
summary: Ericsson is using its own offices and employees to trial 5G laptops as a replacement for corporate Wi-Fi, which could potentially help MNOs tap a new revenue stream.

title: Nokia and Orange push advances in AI‑RAN with NVIDIA AI infrastructure
summary: Nokia and Orange advance Nokia and Orange have expanded their relationship to jointly co‑create and co‑innovate AI‑RAN use cases, supported by NVIDIA AI infrastructure.AI‑RAN innovation with NVIDIA

title: Oracle commits to up to 2.8GW of Bloom's fuel cell power
summary: Bloom’s fuel cells will support Oracle projects in the U.S. and help meet demand for its cloud infrastructure.

title: Mobile ecosystem is facing major memory problems
summary: Telcos may want to start stuffing their piggy banks because a global memory shortage is poised to make network upgrades more expensive than ever.

title: Is permitting reform streamlining or steamrolling?
summary: Permitting reform may soon become law, but several groups claim it stomps on local government control.

title: Equinix exec says in the AI era ‘the network is the agent’
summary: Fabric Intelligence is a new multi-agent networking system that mixes the use of lightweight models for things like pattern recognition and large language models for a conversational interface.

title: Broadband’s sustainability reckoning is happening inside the fiber rollout
summary: As the UK accelerates its full‑fiber rollout, Openreach and Zyxel are reengineering CPE to cut plastic, reduce emissions and push sustainability deeper into the network and supply chain.

title: Trump Mobile’s T1 phone remains MIA
summary: The Trump Mobile phone remains as elusive as ever, but the phone’s design and the Trump Mobile website are sporting new looks this week.

title: Podcast: Data Center 2.0—Compute comes knocking
summary: Welcome to The Five Nine miniseries digging into what's changing in the infrastructure behind modern businesses. Today, we're zeroing in on compute, which chip is best for what, where orgs get tripped up and what matters more than the chip itself.

title: Advice for small wireless and broadband operators who want to tap AI
summary: A panel of wireless experts at CCA yesterday gave advice to small wireless operators who are just starting on their automation and AI journeys.

title: Could AWS’ multi-cloud interconnect spell the end of egress fees?
summary: AWS exec Rob Kennedy noted that the launch of Interconnect – Multicloud provided “an opportunity for us to rethink how we have priced things.”

title: Verizon CEO says ‘best network’ is no longer enough
summary: Verizon CEO Dan Schulman says it’s not enough to claim Verizon has the best network anymore. “We need to invest back in our customers,” he said.

title: Lumen joins AWS to bring the network to the cloud
summary: A Lumen executive told Fierce Network the company is working to replicate the cloud connectivity model with different cloud providers. "This is not something that we envision as being exclusive just with Amazon.”

title: Amazon’s plans for Globalstar’s XCOM RAN remain a mystery
summary: Everyone is focused on Amazon's acquisition of Globalstar's LEO assets, rightly so. But what about XCOM, Globalstar's private 5G product?

title: How Microsoft pushes AI closer to enterprise data
summary: Microsoft is pushing AI closer to enterprise data to unlock real-time insights, balance control and latency, and tap into vast unused data potential. Watch now!

title: Telstra International bets on autonomous networks to keep pace with AI demand
summary: As AI workloads reshape global traffic patterns, Telstra International is rethinking how subsea and terrestrial networks are designed, provisioned and experienced by customers.

title: Churchill Downs names Cisco as networking and infrastructure partner for Kentucky Derby
summary: A network refresh — spanning more than 7,000 switches and a modernized routing fabric — will deliver high-speed, high-capacity connectivity across all CDI properties built for performance at scale for one of the world’s largest sporting events.

title: IBM cybersecurity measures aim to help enterprises battle agentic attacks
summary: IBM Autonomous Security, a new machine speed service composed of IBM AI agents, helps automate vulnerability remediation at a pace humans alone cannot sustain

title: Opinion: Sovereignty tunnels are here
summary: Sovereignty tunnels describe a model in which data remains fixed within a jurisdiction, while access is extended across borders through tightly controlled, policy-driven pathways.

title: Fierce Network wins 2026 bronze medal for B2B industry news coverage
summary: The American Society of Business Publication Editors' 2026 Azbee Awards of Excellence gave Fierce Network a bronze medal in the Northeast Region Award Winners.

title: Data centers undertake great Midwest migration in search of power
summary: Power scarcity is redrawing the U.S. data center map—away from old giants, toward the Midwest. But residents aren't convinced.

title: Infrastructure AI stalls before ROI, research finds
summary: Separate research from Gartner and IDC finds AI adoption in infrastructure and networking isn't meeting expectations.

title: The weirdest AI use cases Fierce Network is watching right now
summary: The Fierce Network editorial team is taking turns writing about strange, surprising and slightly unsettling AI trends and use cases — and then ranking their potential ROI for telcos on a scale of 1-5.

title: Career moves — Proximus board elects Cécile Coune chairwoman
summary: Welcome to Career Moves, Fierce's weekly rundown of recent hirings, firings and retirings across the tech and telecom sectors.
